2606.22375.title: ARIA: A Causal-Aware Framework for Rescuing LLM Reasoning in Trustworthy Materials Discovery
2606.22375.paper_summary: Generative models have revolutionized the process of materials discovery, yet they often fail to satisfy underlying physical causality. Through an analysis of Large Language Models (LLMs) augmented with knowledge graphs derived from current literature, we uncover a phenomenon termed contextual tunneling, where models "over-anchor" on narrow, retrieved evidence while suppressing global physical reasoning. To address this problem, we introduce ARIA, a causal-aware framework that conditions knowledge use on mechanistic completeness. ARIA routes each query through a three-tier cascade: (i) direct causal reasoning when complete evidence chains of Process-Structure-Property (PSP) are available, (ii) physics-informed analogical transfer for sparse or novel material systems, and (iii) explicit parametric fallback when external evidence is incomplete. As a proof of concept, we construct a Knowledge Graph (KG) containing 2,839 extracted PSP relations from peer-reviewed articles in the materials literature and evaluate ARIA on forward prediction and inverse design tasks for two-dimensional (2D) materials. ARIA mitigates contextual tunneling, improves over unaugmented and naive KG-augmented baselines, and provides further gains when an online literature search is used for evidence enrichment. Crucially, ARIA produces auditable causal traces, enabling physically grounded and trustworthy AI-assisted materials discovery.
2606.22375.paper_summary_zh: 生成模型已徹底改變了材料發現的過程，但它們常常無法滿足基本的物理因果關係。通過對增強了來自當前文獻的知識圖譜的大型語言模型（LLMs）的分析，我們揭示了一種稱為上下文隧道效應的現象，模型在狹窄的檢索證據上「過度錨定」，同時抑制了全局物理推理。為了解決這個問題，我們引入了ARIA，一個具備因果意識的框架，該框架將知識的使用條件化為機械完整性。ARIA將每個查詢通過三層級聯路由：（i）當完整的過程-結構-性質（PSP）證據鏈可用時，進行直接因果推理；（ii）對於稀疏或新穎的材料系統，進行物理知識啟發的類比轉移；以及（iii）當外部證據不完整時，進行明確的參數備選。作為概念驗證，我們構建了一個知識圖（KG），其中包含來自材料文獻的2,839個提取的PSP關係，並在二維（2D）材料的前向預測和逆向設計任務上評估ARIA。ARIA減輕了上下文隧道效應，改善了未增強和天真的KG增強基準，並在使用在線文獻搜索進行證據豐富化時提供了進一步的增益。關鍵是，ARIA生成可審計的因果痕跡，使得物理基礎和可信的AI輔助材料發現成為可能。
2606.22375.author: Yi Cao et.al.
2606.22375.authors: Yi Cao, Liaoyaqi Wang, Jieneng Chen, Benjamin Van Durme, Alan Yuille, Paulette Clancy
